title: Forbidden error when sending binary data to URL
symptom: When attempting to upload binary data to a presigned URL, n8n returns a 'Forbidden - perhaps check your credentials?' error.
applies_when: Sending binary data to a presigned URL
not_this_if: Uploading plain/text data works without issues
root_cause: The error occurs because the HTTP request for binary data is not properly configured, potentially due to missing headers or incorrect permissions in the environment settings for the n8n instance.
fix_steps: Ensure the HTTP request is configured to send binary data correctly.
Add the necessary environment variables to your docker-compose.yml file: NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=https,http,url.
agent_instruction: Configure the HTTP Request node to send binary data and update your docker-compose.yml with the required environment variables.
